title: Mephitidia satsumensis
taxonomy: Mephitidia satsumensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Mephitidia and is native to Japan, specifically on the island of Kyushu. The classification of Mephitidia satsumensis is part of the broader taxonomy of the Annonaceae family, which includes over 200 genera and 2,000 species. Mephitidia satsumensis is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within this family. Further research is needed to confirm its classification and understand its unique characteristics.

properties: Mephitidia satsumensis, also known as Japanese pepper or Sansho, has been used in traditional Asian medicine for its various therapeutic properties.
